architecture_summary: Add separate runtime enablement evidence acceptance and runtime admission gate contracts so accepted evidence remains distinct from runtime execution authority.
data_model_changes: Introduce an evidence acceptance gate derived from the runtime enablement review packet with twelve accepted evidence refs and zero authority grants.
Introduce a runtime admission gate derived from the acceptance gate with evidence accepted, runtime admission denied, and Foundation Mode blockers retained.
Register both schemas in the protocol manifest, schema index, CI worker lane, docs, and SDLC artifacts.
implementation_plan: Add evidence acceptance gate schema, fixture, validator, and focused tests.
Add runtime admission gate schema, fixture, validator, and focused tests.
Register both schemas in the protocol manifest and CI worker runtime enablement lane.
Update worker docs, schema README, gap register, and SDLC artifacts.
Run focused validators, focused pytest, protocol manifest validation, SDLC artifact validation, schema validation, and whitespace checks.
invariants: Dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and terminal closure remain denied.
Foundation Mode runtime blockers remain explicit.
rollback_plan: Remove acceptance gate and admission gate schemas, fixtures, validators, tests, manifest entries, CI inclusion, docs updates, and SDLC artifacts.
Restore gap register next action to evidence acceptance and runtime admission gate creation.
tradeoffs: The slice advances evidence acceptance without enabling runtime.
Runtime promotion remains deliberately unresolved and must be handled as a separate governed decision.
